1	Apply a [`tower::Layer`] to the router that will only run if the request matches
2	a route.
3	
4	Note that the middleware is only applied to existing routes. So you have to
5	first add your routes (and / or fallback) and then call `route_layer`
6	afterwards. Additional routes added after `route_layer` is called will not have
7	the middleware added.
8	
9	This works similarly to [`Router::layer`] except the middleware will only run if
10	the request matches a route. This is useful for middleware that return early
11	(such as authorization) which might otherwise convert a `404 Not Found` into a
12	`401 Unauthorized`.
13	
14	This function will panic if no routes have been declared yet on the router,
15	since the new layer will have no effect, and this is typically a bug.
16	In generic code, you can test if that is the case first, by calling [`Router::has_routes`].
17	
18	# Example
19	
20	```rust
21	use axum::{
22	    routing::get,
23	    Router,
24	};
25	use tower_http::validate_request::ValidateRequestHeaderLayer;
26	
27	let app = Router::new()
28	    .route("/foo", get(|| async {}))
29	    .route_layer(ValidateRequestHeaderLayer::bearer("password"));
30	
31	// `GET /foo` with a valid token will receive `200 OK`
32	// `GET /foo` with a invalid token will receive `401 Unauthorized`
33	// `GET /not-found` with a invalid token will receive `404 Not Found`
34	# let _: Router = app;
35	```
36

1	Apply a [`tower::Layer`] to all routes in the router.
2	
3	This can be used to add additional processing to a request for a group
4	of routes.
5	
6	Note that the middleware is only applied to existing routes. So you have to
7	first add your routes (and / or fallback) and then call `layer` afterwards. Additional
8	routes added after `layer` is called will not have the middleware added.
9	
10	If you want to add middleware to a single handler you can either use
11	[`MethodRouter::layer`] or [`Handler::layer`].
12	
13	# Example
14	
15	Adding the [`tower_http::trace::TraceLayer`]:
16	
17	```rust
18	use axum::{routing::get, Router};
19	use tower_http::trace::TraceLayer;
20	
21	let app = Router::new()
22	    .route("/foo", get(|| async {}))
23	    .route("/bar", get(|| async {}))
24	    .layer(TraceLayer::new_for_http());
25	# let _: Router = app;
26	```
27	
28	If you need to write your own middleware see ["Writing
29	middleware"](crate::middleware#writing-middleware) for the different options.
30	
31	If you only want middleware on some routes you can use [`Router::merge`]:
32	
33	```rust
34	use axum::{routing::get, Router};
35	use tower_http::{trace::TraceLayer, compression::CompressionLayer};
36	
37	let with_tracing = Router::new()
38	    .route("/foo", get(|| async {}))
39	    .layer(TraceLayer::new_for_http());
40	
41	let with_compression = Router::new()
42	    .route("/bar", get(|| async {}))
43	    .layer(CompressionLayer::new());
44	
45	// Merge everything into one `Router`
46	let app = Router::new()
47	    .merge(with_tracing)
48	    .merge(with_compression);
49	# let _: Router = app;
50	```
51	
52	# Multiple middleware
53	
54	It's recommended to use [`tower::ServiceBuilder`] when applying multiple
55	middleware. See [`middleware`](crate::middleware) for more details.
56	
57	# Runs after routing
58	
59	Middleware added with this method will run _after_ routing and thus cannot be
60	used to rewrite the request URI. See ["Rewriting request URI in
61	middleware"](crate::middleware#rewriting-request-uri-in-middleware) for more
62	details and a workaround.
63	
64	# Error handling
65	
66	See [`middleware`](crate::middleware) for details on how error handling impacts
67	middleware.

## Step 1 — Tower `Layer<S>` impls inside axum

All `impl Layer<S> for T` sites in the workspace, with the wrapped `S` and produced `Service`:

| Layer type | file:line | Wraps | `type Service` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Extension<T>` | axum/src/extension.rs:140 | `S` (any inner Service) | `AddExtension<S, T>` (axum/src/extension.rs:164, `Service<Request<ResBody>>`) |
| `HandleErrorLayer<F, T>` | axum/src/error_handling/mod.rs:58 | `S` | `HandleError<S, F, T>` |
| `MockConnectInfo<T>` | axum/src/extract/connect_info.rs:208 | `S` | `<Extension<Self> as Layer<S>>::Service` — i.e. delegates to `Extension::layer(inner)` |
| `DefaultBodyLimit` | axum-core/src/extract/default_body_limit.rs:187 | `S` | `DefaultBodyLimitService<S>` |
| `MapRequestLayer<F, S, T>` | axum/src/middleware/map_request.rs:191 | `I` | `MapRequest<F, S, I, T>` |
| `MapResponseLayer<F, S, T>` | axum/src/middleware/map_response.rs:173 | `I` | `MapResponse<F, S, I, T>` |
| `FromFnLayer<F, S, T>` | axum/src/middleware/from_fn.rs:198 | `I` | `FromFn<F, S, I, T>` |
| `FromExtractorLayer<E, S>` | axum/src/middleware/from_extractor.rs:139 | `T` | `FromExtractor<T, E, S>` |
| `ResponseAxumBodyLayer` | axum/src/middleware/response_axum_body.rs:20 | `S` | `ResponseAxumBody<S>` |
| `Either<E1, E2>` (axum-extra) | axum-extra/src/either.rs:277 | `S` (where `E1,E2: Layer<S>`) | `Either<E1::Service, E2::Service>` |

Propagate-style internals (returned anonymously as `impl Layer<S, Service = Self>`, not `impl Layer` on a named type — these are layer **factories** wrapping closures via `tower::layer::layer_fn`):

- `StripPrefix::layer` — axum/src/routing/strip_prefix.rs:17 → `impl Layer<S, Service = StripPrefix<S>>`. The `StripPrefix<S>` itself `impl Service<Request<B>>` at axum/src/routing/strip_prefix.rs:26.
- `SetNestedPath::layer` — axum/src/extract/nested_path.rs:72 → `impl Layer<S, Service = SetNestedPath<S>>`. `SetNestedPath<S>` `impl Service<Request<B>>` at axum/src/extract/nested_path.rs:81. These two are stacked together at axum/src/routing/path_router.rs:231-235 to wrap every nested service.

Notes for next agent:
- `Extension<T>` is **the** canonical "axum-native, Tower-contract" Layer: state stored inside the Layer value, `Service::call` does `req.extensions_mut().insert(self.value.clone())` then delegates. See axum/src/extension.rs:140-187.
- Axum has **no `tower_http::propagate_header`-style** propagate layer of its own; `SetNestedPath`/`StripPrefix` are the only internal "rewrite-extensions-then-delegate" layers, and both are crate-private (`pub(crate)` / `pub(super)`).
- `HandleErrorLayer` is the only public error-conversion Layer in axum proper (axum/src/error_handling/mod.rs:58-67).

## Step 2 — `Router::layer` vs `Router::route_layer` ordering semantics

**Public entry points:**
- `Router::layer` — axum/src/routing/mod.rs:296 (doc: axum/src/docs/routing/layer.md)
- `Router::route_layer` — axum/src/routing/mod.rs:313, `#[track_caller]` (doc: axum/src/docs/routing/route_layer.md)

**The diff is one line each** — both call `map_inner!` but differ in the `catch_all_fallback` field:

```
// Router::layer (mod.rs:304-308)
path_router:        this.path_router.layer(layer.clone()),
catch_all_fallback: this.catch_all_fallback.map(|route| route.layer(layer)),  // <-- wraps fallback too

// Router::route_layer (mod.rs:321-325)
path_router:        this.path_router.route_layer(layer),
catch_all_fallback: this.catch_all_fallback,                                  // <-- fallback untouched
```

**Inside the path router** (axum/src/routing/path_router.rs):
- `PathRouter::layer` (:251) — iterates `self.routes` and calls `endpoint.layer(layer.clone())` on each. No panic if empty.
- `PathRouter::route_layer` (:273) — same iteration, but panics with `"Adding a route_layer before any routes is a no-op…"` if `self.routes.is_empty()` (:281-286). Hence `#[track_caller]` on the public method.

`Endpoint::layer` is at axum/src/routing/mod.rs:796-808, dispatching to:
- `MethodRouter::layer` — axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1014 (changes error type)
- `MethodRouter::route_layer` — axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1044, also `#[track_caller]`, also panics-when-empty (same "no-op" message at :1064)
- `Route::layer` — axum/src/routing/route.rs:60-72 (wraps with `(MapErrLayer::new(Into::into), layer)`, the inner box)

**Practical consequence:**
- `Router::layer(L)` — `L` runs on **every** request that reaches the router, including ones that match no route (they go to `catch_all_fallback`, which is also wrapped). So an auth Layer added with `.layer(...)` converts a `404 Not Found` into a `401 Unauthorized` (because routing decided fallback, then L runs).
- `Router::route_layer(L)` — `L` only wraps the registered route endpoints; the `catch_all_fallback` is **not** wrapped, so unmatched-route requests bypass `L` entirely and produce `404`. This is exactly the doc's stated motivation: "useful for middleware that return early (such as authorization) which might otherwise convert a `404 Not Found` into a `401 Unauthorized`" (route_layer.md:9-12).

**`MethodRouter` analogue is subtler:** `MethodRouter::route_layer` wraps each method slot (get/post/…) but **not** `self.fallback` (method_routing.rs:1069-1079 vs `layer` at :1037 which does `self.fallback.map(layer_fn)`). So a 405 (Method Not Allowed) at the method-router level escapes `route_layer` but is wrapped by `layer`. See the contrasting tests at method_routing.rs:1462 (`layer`) vs :1478 (`route_layer`) — the latter asserts `METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED`, the former `UNAUTHORIZED`.

## Step 3 — Test coverage for layer composition / ordering

- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:738 async fn route_layer` — single Router, `.route_layer(ValidateRequestHeaderLayer::bearer)`. Asserts: matched + bad token → 401; **unmatched route → 404** (the load-bearing assertion at :754); wrong method on matched route → 401 (limitation comment :757-759).
- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:688 async fn middleware_still_run_for_unmatched_requests` — counterpart to above: with `.layer(...)`, custom counter middleware runs on `/not-found` (asserts COUNT==2 at :724). This is the "`.layer` runs for 404" canary.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:330 async fn middleware_applies_to_routes_above` — ordering: only routes added *before* `.layer(...)` get wrapped.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:208 async fn middleware_on_single_route` — `.route(path, get(h).layer(...))` per-route layering.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:851 async fn head_with_middleware_applied` — layer interaction with HEAD-from-GET.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:1117 async fn layer_response_into_response` — `L::Service` response → `IntoResponse` conversion.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:1182 async fn state_isnt_cloned_too_much_in_layer` — middleware composition with state.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:1332 async fn middleware_adding_body` — body rewrite by layer.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:242 async fn outer_middleware_still_see_whole_url` — nested-router propagation: outer `.layer(...)` sees URLs **including** nested-router paths (this is the propagation-into-nested-routes test).
- `axum/src/routing/tests/merge.rs:109 async fn layer` — layer applied before `.merge(...)`.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/merge.rs:126 async fn layer_and_handle_error` — `.layer((HandleErrorLayer::new, TimeoutLayer))` composition through merge.
- `axum/src/routing/tests/merge.rs:373` (in test starting earlier) — auth `.layer(ValidateRequestHeaderLayer)` applied to a sub-router that is then merged with a public one.
- `axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1462 async fn layer` (in `mod tests`) — `MethodRouter::layer`: method with no route → 401 (Layer wraps the 405 path).
- `axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1478 async fn route_layer` (same mod) — `MethodRouter::route_layer`: method with no route → **405** (Layer does NOT wrap the 405 path). These two are the cleanest fixture for the "skip on no-match" behavior.
- `axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:204-357` — the cluster of `cannot_extract_nested_matched_path_in_middleware*` / `can_extract_…` tests precisely exercise extension-propagation across nested routers and `.layer(map_request(...))` boundaries. These are the model for any per-request extractor that needs to span nested routers.

## Step 4 — Adding a built-in `RequestId` Layer: edit map

**Architectural precedents to copy from:**
- `Extension<T>` (axum/src/extension.rs:1-187) for the **Layer + Service + extractor** wiring: the Layer impl (:140), the wrapping Service `AddExtension<S, T>` with `req.extensions_mut().insert(...)` (:169-187), and `FromRequestParts` + `OptionalFromRequestParts` (:83, :100).
- `MatchedPath` (axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs) for the **per-request, nested-router-aware extension** pattern: `MatchedPath(Arc<str>)` (:57), `FromRequestParts` (:67), the **propagation rule** in `append_nested_matched_path` (:125-140) — when an outer router has already set the value, the inner router *appends* rather than overwrites.

**Implementation choice — recommendation: bespoke Service wrapper, NOT Extension-style.**
- `Extension<T>::layer` clones a fixed value `T` for every request. RequestId must *generate* a new UUID per `Service::call`, so the Layer can't carry the value.
- Modeled on `AddExtension`: a `SetRequestId<S>` Service whose `call` (i) checks `req.extensions().get::<RequestId>()` (preserve from upstream — supports the "client supplied X-Request-Id" / "nested router already set one" propagation contract), (ii) otherwise generates a new UUID and inserts it, (iii) needs to also set the response header, which means the future has to project the header onto the response — see `RouteFuture` in axum/src/routing/route.rs:108-117 for the pin-projected response-mutation pattern, or `MapResponse` (axum/src/middleware/map_response.rs) for a closer template.

**Files to touch:**

| File:line | Change |
|---|---|
| **NEW** `axum/src/request_id.rs` | Define `RequestId(Uuid)` (Clone+Copy+Debug), `RequestIdLayer` (Clone+Default; optional `header_name` config), `SetRequestId<S>` service, `SetRequestIdFuture<F>` (pin-projected, captures the id, on poll inserts `x-request-id` header into response). Plus `FromRequestParts` / `OptionalFromRequestParts` impls — same shape as axum/src/extension.rs:83-113. |
| `axum/src/lib.rs:512` | `pub use self::request_id::{RequestId, RequestIdLayer};` next to the existing `pub use self::extension::Extension;`. Add `pub mod request_id;` near where `mod extension;` lives. |
| `axum/src/extract/mod.rs:47` | `pub use crate::RequestId;` next to existing `pub use crate::Extension;`. |
| `axum/src/middleware/mod.rs:24` | Re-export `SetRequestId` next to `pub use crate::extension::AddExtension;`. |
| `axum/src/extract/rejection.rs` (around :142 where `ExtensionRejection`/`MissingExtension` live) | Add `MissingRequestId` rejection variant — used if a handler asks for `RequestId` but the layer wasn't installed. Mirrors the `MissingExtension` shape exactly. |
| `axum/Cargo.toml` | Add `uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4"], optional = true }` and a `request-id` feature (gate the module behind `cfg(feature = "request-id")` consistent with `matched-path` gating at axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:55). Update `axum/src/lib.rs` cfg gates. |
| **NEW** `axum/src/docs/request_id.md` | User-facing doc, included via `#[doc = include_str!(...)]` on `RequestIdLayer` — same convention as axum/src/docs/routing/layer.md. |
| Tests in `axum/src/request_id.rs` (test module) | Cover: (1) header is set on every response; (2) handler can extract `RequestId`; (3) **client-supplied `X-Request-Id` is preserved** (propagation contract); (4) **nested router does not overwrite outer id** — copy structure from axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:204-357; (5) `OptionalFromRequestParts` returns `None` without the layer (mirror axum/src/extension.rs:209). |

**Propagation contract with nested routers (the load-bearing decision):**
- The layer should be **idempotent against an existing `RequestId` in `req.extensions()`** — see `append_nested_matched_path` (axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:125-140) for the exact pattern: peek the extension first, only insert if absent.
- This makes installation **safe at any level** (outer router, inner nested router, or both) — matches user expectations that ids flow through `Router::nest`/`Router::merge`. Tests at axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:242 (`outer_middleware_still_see_whole_url`) and the matched-path nested cluster are the precedent fixtures to model.
- Document explicitly that this Layer should be installed via `Router::layer` (not `route_layer`), so unmatched-route 404 responses **do** get a request id header (consistent with the `.layer` vs `.route_layer` semantics traced in Step 2).

## Step 2 — `Router::layer` vs `Router::route_layer` ordering semantics

**Public entry points:**
- `Router::layer` — axum/src/routing/mod.rs:296 (doc: axum/src/docs/routing/layer.md)
- `Router::route_layer` — axum/src/routing/mod.rs:313, `#[track_caller]` (doc: axum/src/docs/routing/route_layer.md)

**The diff is one line each** — both call `map_inner!` but differ in the `catch_all_fallback` field:

```
// Router::layer (mod.rs:304-308)
path_router:        this.path_router.layer(layer.clone()),
catch_all_fallback: this.catch_all_fallback.map(|route| route.layer(layer)),  // <-- wraps fallback too

// Router::route_layer (mod.rs:321-325)
path_router:        this.path_router.route_layer(layer),
catch_all_fallback: this.catch_all_fallback,                                  // <-- fallback untouched
```

**Inside the path router** (axum/src/routing/path_router.rs):
- `PathRouter::layer` (:251) — iterates `self.routes` and calls `endpoint.layer(layer.clone())` on each. No panic if empty.
- `PathRouter::route_layer` (:273) — same iteration, but panics with `"Adding a route_layer before any routes is a no-op…"` if `self.routes.is_empty()` (:281-286). Hence `#[track_caller]` on the public method.

`Endpoint::layer` is at axum/src/routing/mod.rs:796-808, dispatching to:
- `MethodRouter::layer` — axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1014 (changes error type)
- `MethodRouter::route_layer` — axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1044, also `#[track_caller]`, also panics-when-empty (same "no-op" message at :1064)
- `Route::layer` — axum/src/routing/route.rs:60-72 (wraps with `(MapErrLayer::new(Into::into), layer)`, the inner box)

**Practical consequence:**
- `Router::layer(L)` — `L` runs on **every** request that reaches the router, including ones that match no route (they go to `catch_all_fallback`, which is also wrapped). So an auth Layer added with `.layer(...)` converts a `404 Not Found` into a `401 Unauthorized` (because routing decided fallback, then L runs).
- `Router::route_layer(L)` — `L` only wraps the registered route endpoints; the `catch_all_fallback` is **not** wrapped, so unmatched-route requests bypass `L` entirely and produce `404`. This is exactly the doc's stated motivation: "useful for middleware that return early (such as authorization) which might otherwise convert a `404 Not Found` into a `401 Unauthorized`" (route_layer.md:9-12).

**`MethodRouter` analogue is subtler:** `MethodRouter::route_layer` wraps each method slot (get/post/…) but **not** `self.fallback` (method_routing.rs:1069-1079 vs `layer` at :1037 which does `self.fallback.map(layer_fn)`). So a 405 (Method Not Allowed) at the method-router level escapes `route_layer` but is wrapped by `layer`. See the contrasting tests at method_routing.rs:1462 (`layer`) vs :1478 (`route_layer`) — the latter asserts `METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED`, the former `UNAUTHORIZED`.

- `axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:738 async fn route_layer` — single Router, `.route_layer(ValidateRequestHeaderLayer::bearer)`. Asserts: matched + bad token → 401; **unmatched route → 404** (the load-bearing assertion at :754); wrong method on matched route → 401 (limitation comment :757-759).

## Step 4 — Adding a built-in `RequestId` Layer: edit map

**Architectural precedents to copy from:**
- `Extension<T>` (axum/src/extension.rs:1-187) for the **Layer + Service + extractor** wiring: the Layer impl (:140), the wrapping Service `AddExtension<S, T>` with `req.extensions_mut().insert(...)` (:169-187), and `FromRequestParts` + `OptionalFromRequestParts` (:83, :100).
- `MatchedPath` (axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs) for the **per-request, nested-router-aware extension** pattern: `MatchedPath(Arc<str>)` (:57), `FromRequestParts` (:67), the **propagation rule** in `append_nested_matched_path` (:125-140) — when an outer router has already set the value, the inner router *appends* rather than overwrites.

**Implementation choice — recommendation: bespoke Service wrapper, NOT Extension-style.**
- `Extension<T>::layer` clones a fixed value `T` for every request. RequestId must *generate* a new UUID per `Service::call`, so the Layer can't carry the value.
- Modeled on `AddExtension`: a `SetRequestId<S>` Service whose `call` (i) checks `req.extensions().get::<RequestId>()` (preserve from upstream — supports the "client supplied X-Request-Id" / "nested router already set one" propagation contract), (ii) otherwise generates a new UUID and inserts it, (iii) needs to also set the response header, which means the future has to project the header onto the response — see `RouteFuture` in axum/src/routing/route.rs:108-117 for the pin-projected response-mutation pattern, or `MapResponse` (axum/src/middleware/map_response.rs) for a closer template.

| Tests in `axum/src/request_id.rs` (test module) | Cover: (1) header is set on every response; (2) handler can extract `RequestId`; (3) **client-supplied `X-Request-Id` is preserved** (propagation contract); (4) **nested router does not overwrite outer id** — copy structure from axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:204-357; (5) `OptionalFromRequestParts` returns `None` without the layer (mirror axum/src/extension.rs:209). |

**Propagation contract with nested routers (the load-bearing decision):**
- The layer should be **idempotent against an existing `RequestId` in `req.extensions()`** — see `append_nested_matched_path` (axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:125-140) for the exact pattern: peek the extension first, only insert if absent.
- This makes installation **safe at any level** (outer router, inner nested router, or both) — matches user expectations that ids flow through `Router::nest`/`Router::merge`. Tests at axum/src/routing/tests/mod.rs:242 (`outer_middleware_still_see_whole_url`) and the matched-path nested cluster are the precedent fixtures to model.
- Document explicitly that this Layer should be installed via `Router::layer` (not `route_layer`), so unmatched-route 404 responses **do** get a request id header (consistent with the `.layer` vs `.route_layer` semantics traced in Step 2).
